summary:
- publisher=Reuters, title=Strait of Hormuz shipping at a standstill despite latest US pledge, directness=high, why=confirms persistent disruption despite high-level U.S. intervention, next=https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/most-strait-hormuz-shipping-standstill-despite-latest-us-pledge-2026-05-04/  
- publisher=Reuters, title=Iranian oil flows through Strait of Hormuz even as Gulf neighbors' exports shut, directness=high, why=contradicts closure claims; shows Iran continues transit despite regional tensions, next=http://today.reuters.com/business/energy/iranian-oil-flows-through-strait-hormuz-even-gulf-neighbors-exports-shut-2026-03-11/  
- publisher=Reuters, title=Chinese oil tankers transit Strait of Hormuz data shows, directness=high, why=provides empirical evidence of ongoing traffic despite U.S.-Iran conflict, next=https://uk.reuters.com/business/energy/chinese-oil-tankers-transit-strait-hormuz-data-shows-2026-04-11/  
- publisher=Reuters, title=Gulf's fragile trade lifeline hangs on two eastern UAE ports, directness=high, why=identifies Fujairah and Khor Fakkan as critical rerouting hubs due to Hormuz disruption, next=https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/gulfs-fragile-trade-lifeline-hangs-two-eastern-uae-ports-2026-05-06/  
- publisher=Reuters, title=OPEC+ agrees to boost oil output when Strait of Hormuz reopens, directness=high, why=links future supply decisions to Hormuz reopening—key forecasting trigger, next=https://in.mobile.reuters.com/business/energy/opec-debates-theoretical-oil-output-hike-amid-iran-war-paralysis-sources-say-2026-04-05/  
- publisher=Reuters, title=Hormuz shutdown worsens after US hits Iranian warship, directness=medium, why=confirms escalation event (U.S. sinking Iranian vessel) but conflates stranded tankers with closure; verify if incident triggered or resulted from standstill, next=https://today.reuters.com/business/energy/hormuz-shutdown-worsens-after-us-hits-iranian-warship-tankers-stranded-fifth-day-2026-03-04/  
- publisher=Reuters, title=Oil climbs as Iran vows to keep Strait of Hormuz closed, directness=medium, why=Iran’s stated intent to close contrasts with observed traffic—verify if vow is rhetorical or enforced, next=http://today.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-climbs-tankers-are-attacked-iraqi-waters-amid-middle-east-war-2026-03-12/
